title: Photos: See breathtaking tall ships sail toward Boston
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Over 60 international tall ships and military vessels will arrive in Boston Harbor for the Sail Boston event starting July 11.
The German tall ship, Gorch Folk, leads the formation for the Five Sisters Race toward the starting line on July 9, 2026.
Boston marks the final stop of the Sail250 tour, ending the five Sisters Tall Ship Race from New York to Boston.

title: Patriots 2026 position preview: Tight ends edition
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Hunter Henry returns as the only experienced tight end, coming off one of his best seasons with 60 catches for 768 yards.
Rookies Eli Raridon and Tanner Arkin join the Patriots' tight end group, with Raridon's size and speed making him one to watch.
Depth behind Henry is limited; former fullback Jack Westover and practice squad member CJ Dippre provide additional options.

title: Q&A: Gigi Perez reflects on Berklee, Boston, and a special Fenway week alongside Noah Kahan
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: Gigi Perez gained fame with 'Sailor Song,' which topped charts and went viral on TikTok in 2024.
Perez is currently supporting Noah Kahan on 'The Great Divide' Tour, performing at Fenway Park this week.
Perez briefly attended Berklee College of Music, which significantly influenced her career despite facing personal challenges.

title: Rideshare passenger attacked by driver en route to Logan, police say
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: A rideshare passenger reported being assaulted by a driver before being dropped at Logan International Airport, police said.
The incident, described as a stabbing, occurred early Friday; the rideshare driver left the scene, authorities stated.
The passenger sustained non-life-threatening injuries and was hospitalized; no arrests have been made, and the investigation continues.

title: How a chance encounter with a Scotland fan outside Fenway Park became a viral love story
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Jill Roberts and Tommy Morrison met outside Fenway Park after a Red Sox game and have a viral love story.
The couple's first encounter video has over 620,000 views on TikTok, sparking widespread media attention.
Roberts plans to visit Morrison in Scotland this summer as they maintain their long-distance relationship.

title: Look inside a new waterfront hotel on Lake Champlain
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: The Harborvale, Autograph Collection opened as a 161-room boutique hotel on Lake Champlain in Burlington, Vermont.
Developed by Westport Hospitality, the hotel features a restaurant, pool, 3,000 sq. ft. event space, and fitness center.
Rooms range from $299 to $499+ per night; the property is dog-friendly and offers both valet and onsite parking.

title: Woman charged after deadly pedestrian crash on Nantucket
author: John Waller
bullets: Kathleen M. Featherston, 62, was charged with motor vehicle homicide after a fatal crash in Nantucket's Stop & Shop parking lot.
The accident on June 29 resulted in the death of Stacey J. Rosen, 62, from Atlantic Beach, New York.
Charges were delayed due to the time taken to obtain evidence from the vehicle's manufacturer, according to Nantucket police.

title: Trump denies Mass. disaster relief for Feb. blizzard; Healey says she will appeal
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: President Trump denied Massachusetts' request for federal disaster relief following a February blizzard that dumped 3 feet of snow.
Massachusetts Governor Maura Healey criticized the denial and plans to appeal for aid covering snow removal and storm costs.
Trump is accused of politicizing disaster aid approvals, favoring Republican-leaning states over those with Democratic leadership.

title: Local obituary: Donato Ventura, aka Danny V., longtime Boston Herald high school sports reporter
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: Donato Ventura, known as Danny V., passed away at 66 after battling cancer.
Ventura worked over 35 years as a high school sports reporter for the Boston Herald.
Funeral services will be held in Brighton with burial at Newton Cemetery & Arboretum.

title: Boxford woman linked to two other embezzlement cases charged with stealing thousands from employer
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Stephanie Hirshon, 53, charged with stealing over $120,000 from employer, linked to other embezzlement cases.
Hirshon previously pleaded guilty to $50,000 theft from Framingham Chamber of Commerce; on probation for that case.
Police arrested Hirshon after finding unauthorized payments of $17,500 to her account; hearing set for August 7.
